title: aion_kit/template: composition primitives and named-template render
purpose: Delivers the text/prompt rendering module: the composition primitives and a named-template render, generalised from the prompts.gleam join_blocks/join_lines/bulleted helpers and its key-value lines (files_line/reference_line). This is how a family composes prompt text from parts — joining sections, lines, bullets, and key-value pairs while dropping the empties — without re-deriving the joiners (S1), and how a static prompt skeleton can carry its holes as named placeholders (S2). Pure and deterministic (CN2): safe to call from workflow or activity code. Depends on KIT-001 only for the package skeleton; it shares no files with KIT-003 and is parallel-runnable with it once KIT-001 lands.
task: Create gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/template.gleam and gleam/aion_kit/test/template_test.gleam. Generalise the prompts.gleam composition primitives into pure module functions: join_blocks(blocks) joining sections with a blank line between them and dropping empty-string sections; join_lines(lines) joining with single newlines and dropping empty-string lines; bulleted(items) rendering each item as a '- item' line joined by newlines; kv(key, value) rendering a single 'key: value' line (the generalisation of files_line/reference_line's key-value shape). Add render(template, bindings): substitute every '{name}' placeholder in the template string from a list of (name, value) bindings; a placeholder with no matching binding is left verbatim (no invented default — ADR-001, C12); every occurrence of a bound name is substituted. The behaviour must match what prompts.gleam relies on exactly (empty-section dropping, empty-line dropping). In scope: the template module, its tests, and the purity guarantee for the file this brief adds (re-verifying C17/C18's properties for template.gleam, which KIT-001 owns as cluster checklist items — split noted). Out of scope: payload (KIT-001), json wrangling (KIT-003), and any reshape of brief_dev's prompts.gleam (RM-023). aion_kit/template does not depend on prompts.gleam and does not import it; it is the standalone generalisation prompts.gleam will later be reshaped to consume.

requirements.title: Section and line joiners
acceptance: cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs template_test and the joiner cases pass.

requirements.title: Bulleted lists and key-value lines
requirements.spec: Add bulleted and kv to gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/template.gleam. WHEN bulleted(items) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL render each item as a line prefixed with '- ' and join the lines with single newlines. WHEN kv(key, value) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the single line '<key>: <value>'. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT drop, sort, or deduplicate items in bulleted (order is preserved verbatim), and SHALL NOT trim the key or value in kv. bulleted([]) SHALL return the empty string.
acceptance: bulleted preserves input order: bulleted(["b", "a"]) == "- b\n- a".
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs template_test and the bulleted/kv cases pass.

requirements.title: Named-template render with explicit binding behaviour
requirements.spec: Add render to gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/template.gleam. WHEN render(template, bindings) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL replace every occurrence of each '{name}' placeholder in the template with the value bound to name in the bindings list, leaving text containing no placeholder unchanged. IF a placeholder's name has no matching binding, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL leave that placeholder verbatim in the output and SHALL NOT substitute an invented empty or default value (ADR-001, C12). IF a binding's name does not occur in the template, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL leave the output otherwise unchanged (an unused binding is not an error). THE SYSTEM SHALL substitute every occurrence of a bound placeholder, not only the first.
acceptance: render("Hello {who}, run {n} times", [("who", "world"), ("n", "3")]) == "Hello world, run 3 times".
render("keep {missing} as is", []) == "keep {missing} as is" (unmatched placeholder left verbatim).
render("{x} and {x}", [("x", "q")]) == "q and q" (every occurrence substituted).
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs template_test and the render cases (including the unmatched-placeholder case) pass.

boundaries: SHALL NOT create or modify aion_kit/payload.gleam or aion_kit/json.gleam — those are KIT-001 and KIT-003.
SHALL NOT import or modify examples/stacked-dev/src/stacked_dev/prompts.gleam — template is the standalone generalisation; reshaping prompts.gleam to consume it is RM-023.
SHALL NOT add a templating expression/logic language (conditionals, loops, filters) to render — only named '{name}' substitution; control flow stays ordinary Gleam in the caller (design non-goal).
SHALL NOT add a default value, escaping policy, or limit for unmatched placeholders beyond leaving them verbatim (ADR-001).
SHALL NOT import aion/workflow, aion/activity, aion/query, aion/signal, or perform any IO (CN2).
verification: cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam build && gleam test && gleam format --check all exit 0 (template suite runs).
grep -E 'panic|let assert' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/template.gleam produces no matches in library code (re-verifying C18's purity for the file this brief adds; C18 is owned by KIT-001).
grep -F 'prompts' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/template.gleam produces no matches (template imports nothing from the example).
gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/template.gleam is at or under 500 code lines (excluding comments and whitespace).
